authoritative_sources.title: Special Conditions for Architect/Engineer Services (AE110)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Smithsonian is a public owner with formal A/E service conditions. The document is authoritative for A/E scope, submissions, site visits, cost-estimate coordination, governmental-agency coordination, and licensed design responsibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Scope, submission, estimate, and authority framing

authoritative_sources.title: Design Submission Instructions: Major Construction Design Review Procedure
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs via WBDG
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: VA major-construction design review procedure is authoritative for formal design-review checkpoints, review packaging, and submission expectations in public A/E project delivery.
used_for: handoff boundaries around unresolved review cycles

authoritative_sources.title: Project management plan - full or condensed version
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's official PMIS training is authoritative for what the project management plan records, who updates it, and how project strategy, review cadence, and scope details are maintained in the workflow system.

authoritative_sources.title: Client Enrichment Series
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official GSA PMIS guide is authoritative for the project-system applications clients and delivery teams actually use for project information, submittals, punch lists, RFIs, meetings, documents, and collaborative tasks.
used_for: artifact routing between projects, RFIs, submittals, punch lists, and meetings

authoritative_sources.title: P120 Project Cost and Schedule Management Requirements for the Public Buildings Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA P120 is an official cost and schedule control directive that governs baselines, updates, milestones, and estimate posture in a real owner-side project delivery environment.
used_for: evidence needed before stating delivery posture

authoritative_sources.title: GSA Commissioning Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. General Services Administration via WBDG
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA commissioning guidance is authoritative for Owner's Project Requirements, Basis of Design alignment, submittal review, issue logs, test procedures, record documents, and closeout controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Commissioning, testing, and closeout evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Building Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This AHJ inspection quick sheet is authoritative for a public final-inspection baseline requiring approved plans on site, associated permits complete, and special inspection letters approved before completion is represented.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and minimum-facts validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm project identity, phase, discipline, jurisdiction, contract or task-order context, system-of-record availability, and approval boundary before a delivery answer is produced.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Design, estimate, and project-control evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect the project management plan, design submissions, review comments, estimate basis, schedule baseline, meeting records, RFIs, and submittal status needed to determine current delivery posture.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Permit, field-readiness, and approval boundary screening
workflow_stages.description: Check approved plans, permits, inspection prerequisites, site access, and authority thresholds before describing the work as ready to proceed.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Support resolution and stakeholder packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare a resolution note, stakeholder communication summary, or project or field-controls summary that preserves A/E terminology, cites blockers, and names next owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Commissioning, closeout, and turnover blocker handling
workflow_stages.description: Require commissioning issues, record documents, O&M manuals, training records, inspection prerequisites, and closeout evidence before completion or turnover is represented as complete.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception escalation and adjacent-owner transfer
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported exceptions, repeated delivery failures, licensed-boundary questions, and permitting or design-quality issues to the correct owner with an explicit evidence bundle.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: project charters, scopes of work, task orders, and project management plans
basis-of-design documents, design-submission packages, design review comments, and interdisciplinary coordination records
estimate packages, milestone schedules, progress reports, risk registers, and decision logs
RFI logs, submittal packages, meeting minutes, field coordination notes, and issue trackers
permit records, approved plan sets, AHJ correspondence, inspection records, and special inspection letters
commissioning plans, issue logs, test procedures, record documents, and closeout checklists
punch lists, as-builts, O&M manuals, training records, and turnover packages
resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: common data environment or document control repository
permit tracking portals and inspection systems
tenant authority matrices and policy libraries

allowed: Classify engineering project delivery requests as supported, blocked on missing prerequisites, or escalated due to authority or evidence limits.
Request the minimum tenant facts, records, and system-of-record context needed to summarize documented project state safely.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, project or field-controls summaries, and escalation packets grounded in the controlling records.
escalate: Licensed architect or engineer review, permit approval, code interpretation, commercial approval, or safety-critical authority changes the answer.
Design review status, estimate posture, permit state, or closeout readiness is materially disputed or contradictory across sources.
A repeated delivery failure, unresolved review cycle, or cross-functional blocker requires a specialized adjacent lane or human owner.
refuse: Sign, seal, certify, or otherwise provide licensed design authority
Approve permits, design deviations, cost commitments, change-order money, or safety-critical release
Describe the package as ready, approved, issued for construction, or complete without sufficient supporting evidence

domain_failure_modes: answering like a generic project coordinator and ignoring basis-of-design, design-review, estimate-control, or licensed-review artifacts
describing work as ready to proceed even though permit, approved-plan, site-access, or inspection prerequisites remain incomplete
collapsing project delivery support into licensed architect or engineer judgment, permit approval, or code interpretation
treating schedule or estimate summaries as approvals rather than controlled evidence-backed status
marking closeout or turnover complete while commissioning issues, record documents, O&M manuals, or final inspection prerequisites remain open

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant contract-form and design-phase naming differences
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources use owner-specific phase names and submission milestones. Tenant retrieval must map those names to the local contract form and approved delivery stages before runtime use.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit and closeout artifact sets
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: AHJ guidance varies by jurisdiction. The public baseline proves the class of artifacts needed, but tenant retrieval must supply the governing jurisdiction and permit workflow before confident permit or closeout messaging.
